message:
audit: fcp-raptorq clean — encode/decode bounds verified, no shippable findings

Surveyed encode.rs, decode.rs, chunk.rs, config.rs after recent
hardening (9ce1cacc decode bounds, 04c9ea7a ingress validation,
74d62e6e numeric overflow, adversarial-flood cap).

Encoder: amplification bounded structurally — repair_ratio_bps is
typed u16 (max 65535), capping max repair symbols at ~370k for
K=56403, total ~13MB Vec::with_capacity in encode_all. K itself is
bounded by encode.rs:68 (max 56403). Payload bounded by
max_object_size before any work happens.

Decoder: 9ce1cacc fix added comprehensive bounds via
validate_decode_bounds (transfer_length <= max_object_size, k bounded
by source_symbols(max_object_size)), validate_symbol_ingress (size
match, non-zero block), validate_duplicate_symbol (byte-equal
duplicate vs adversarial conflict — distinguishes the two failure
modes for observability), validate_direct_buffer_capacity (BTreeMap
cap). DecodeAdmissionController tracks per-permit memory + symbols
buffered + timeout + max_concurrent.

reconstruct: Vec::with_capacity(transfer_len) bounded by validated
transfer_len. try_reconstruct_dense's overdetermined-system rows
bounded by params.l + received.len() (params.l bounded by k via
RaptorQ params).

Chunk module: reconstruct allocates capacity = sum of input chunk
sizes, not an amplification (caller already paid). from_payload's
chunk_count = payload.len() / chunk_size; with pathologically small
chunk_size could be huge, but chunk_size is operator-config not
attacker-controlled — misconfig hazard, not an attack vector. Worth
a defensive max_chunks cap eventually but not security-grade.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
